skill.name: agile-product-owner
skill.description: Create INVEST-style user stories, acceptance criteria, sprint plans, backlog summaries, and lightweight agile delivery metrics.
skill.summary: Generate structured agile product owner artifacts for backlog and sprint planning.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings were false positives. The cited lines contain product-owner text, string processing, and sample epic data, with no cryptographic API usage. The external command findings are documentation examples for running a local Python script, so the skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported weak cryptography at these locations, including a duplicate alert at scripts/user_story_generator.py line 57. Review found no cryptographic imports, hash calls, encryption, or password handling at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code is plain product-owner content and deterministic string handling. No evidence of cryptographic operations exists in the reviewed lines.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Commands
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported external command patterns in the usage section. These are Markdown examples showing how a user may run the bundled local Python script, not code that executes shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear only inside documentation usage bullets. The Python script itself does not call subprocess, shell, network, environment, or filesystem APIs.

content.value_statement: Product teams often lose time turning epics into clear delivery work. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured stories, criteria, and sprint plans.
actual_capabilities: Breaks a sample epic into user stories for multiple personas.
Creates acceptance criteria using clear Given, When, Then style phrasing.
Checks each story against INVEST criteria.
Estimates story points from simple complexity indicators.
Builds a sprint plan from capacity and prioritized backlog items.
Prints readable backlog and sprint summaries from the included script.
limitations: Uses heuristic story generation and does not replace product discovery.
Runs on sample data unless the script is adapted for real backlog input.
Does not integrate directly with Jira, Azure DevOps, or Linear.
Story point estimates need team review before sprint commitment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Epic Breakdown
use_cases.description: Turn a product epic into candidate stories with priorities, points, and acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Plan Sprint Capacity
use_cases.description: Select committed and stretch work from a prioritized backlog based on available team capacity.

use_cases.title: Review Story Quality
use_cases.description: Check whether proposed backlog items are independent, valuable, estimable, small, and testable.

prompt_templates.title: Create Stories from an Epic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agile product owner skill to break this epic into user stories with acceptance criteria: [paste epic].

prompt_templates.title: Improve Story Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these user stories against INVEST criteria. Rewrite weak items and explain what changed: [paste stories].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Sprint
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sprint plan for a team capacity of [points]. Include committed work, stretch items, and risks: [paste backlog].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Backlog
prompt_templates.prompt: Group these epics into a release backlog. Generate stories, dependencies, priorities, and sprint sequencing assumptions: [paste roadmap].

output_examples.input: Epic: Create a user dashboard with metrics, exports, sharing, and automated reports.
output_examples.output: A backlog with dashboard stories for end users and power users, acceptance criteria, priority labels, story points, and INVEST checks.

output_examples.input: Sprint capacity: 30 points with a dashboard backlog.
output_examples.output: A sprint plan showing committed stories, stretch goals, total committed points, and capacity utilization.

output_examples.input: Story: Users can somehow get better reports.
output_examples.output: A refined story with clearer user value, measurable acceptance criteria, and notes about weak testability.

best_practices: Provide real personas, business goals, and constraints before generating stories.
Review generated acceptance criteria with engineering and quality teams.
Use story point estimates as a planning input, not as a final commitment.
anti_patterns: Do not accept generated stories without product and technical review.
Do not use vague epics when clear scope and outcomes are available.
Do not treat the sprint plan as a replacement for team capacity discussion.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates user stories, acceptance criteria, INVEST checks, point estimates, and sprint planning summaries.

faq.question: Does it connect to agile tracking tools?
faq.answer: No. It does not directly connect to Jira, Azure DevOps, Linear, or similar systems.

faq.question: Can it use my real backlog?
faq.answer: Yes, an AI assistant can adapt the skill guidance to your pasted backlog or epic details.

faq.question: Are the estimates final?
faq.answer: No. Estimates are heuristic and should be reviewed by the delivery team.

faq.question: Is the included script required?
faq.answer: No. The script is useful for local examples, but the guidance can also support direct AI-assisted planning.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
